title: FedEx (FDX)Intrinsic Value
description: We ran a full discounted cash flow on FedEx using FY2025 actuals. One number. Every assumption disclosed. Here's what FedEx is really worth.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale:
">
10Y Treasury: 4.18%. FedEx is highly sensitive to global economic cycles, fuel prices, and labor markets. A 9.5% discount rate represents the required return to compensate investors for the economic cyclicality and capital intensity inherent in a global airline and trucking fleet.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale:
">
Logistics is a physical business requiring physical assets. We assume 2.5% terminal growth, slightly below standard nominal GDP growth, to account for the continuous capital expenditure required to maintain planes, hubs, and vehicles in perpetuity.

verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 18, 2026).

faq.question: Why use a 9.5% discount rate?
faq.answer: Westmount Research. "FedEx (FDX) Intrinsic Value: A DCF Analysis." westmountfundamentals.com, March 18, 2026.
